executive_summary: Global markets experience relief rally with Wall Street unwinding war-related positioning as stocks, dollar, and fear indices normalize. Tech stocks lead with Nasdaq setting new records while higher oil prices and yields fail to dent US equity momentum amid reduced rate cut expectations.

key_insights.insight: Wall Street war premium unwinds completely - stocks rally, dollar normalizes, and fear index drops sharply as geopolitical risk premium dissipates
Nasdaq reaches record highs as investors rotate back into technology stocks, signaling renewed confidence in growth sector
SEC appoints David Woodcock as Director of Division of Enforcement, signaling potential intensification of regulatory enforcement actions
US stocks resilient despite higher oil prices and yields - markets price out further Fed rate cuts amid inflation persistence
SEC approves cross-margining in US Treasury market, fundamentally altering margin requirements and liquidity dynamics
Federal Reserve terminates enforcement actions with Crédit Agricole, Mega International Commercial Bank, and Goldman Sachs, resolving legacy compliance issues
Fed FOMC minutes reveal March 2026 policy discussions, providing insight into rate path and quantitative tightening considerations
Pakistan seeks guarantees for second US-Iran talks, reducing Middle East geopolitical risk premium and easing energy supply concerns
Strait of Hormuz security concerns persist despite diplomatic progress, maintaining tail risk for global oil supply and shipping
Fed discount rate meeting minutes show board assessment of financial conditions and regional bank stability

blind_spot: Federal Reserve appears to be winding down enforcement era with major bank settlements, potentially signaling shift in regulatory focus from enforcement to innovation and digital asset integration, which could reshape competitive landscape for traditional banks.
